name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_MEMORY_SAVER_MODE_SETTING_DESCRIPTION
context: Description for the memory saver mode setting
message: Nitrous frees up memory from inactive tabs. This gives active tabs and other apps more computer resources and keeps Nitrous fast. Your inactive tabs automatically become active again when you go back to them.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_BATTERY_SAVER_MODE_SETTING_DESCRIPTION
context: Description for the energy saver mode setting
message: Nitrous conserves battery power by limiting background activity and visual effects, such as smooth scrolling and video frame rates.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_PRELOAD_TOGGLE_SUMMARY
context: Summary for the preload pages setting
message: Nitrous preloads pages which makes browsing and searching faster.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_TAB_DISCARDING_EXCEPTIONS_ADD_DIALOG_HELP
context: Help text shown on the second tab of the tab discarding exception list add dialog. Explains how to use the filter format to add an exclusion rule to the list.
message: Sites you add will always stay active and memory won't be freed up from them.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_DISCARD_RING_TREATMENT_ENABLED_DESCRIPTION_WITH_LEARN_LINK
context: Label for a performance settings toggle description that controls whether the inactive tab UI is shown. Also contains a link to the help center article.
message: A dotted circle appears around site icons.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_INTERVENTION_NOTIFICATION_ENABLED_DESCRIPTION
context: Label for a performance settings toggle that controls whether performance intervention notifications should be shown and link to learn more about performance issue alerts.
message: Get notifications that suggest ways to improve detected performance issues.

name: IDS_IMPORT_FULL_DISK_ACCESS_DIALOG_TITLE
context: Title for the dialog shown when Full Disk Access is needed to import from Safari.
message: Full Disk Access required

name: IDS_IMPORT_FULL_DISK_ACCESS_DIALOG_MESSAGE
context: Message for the dialog shown when Full Disk Access is needed to import bookmarks from Safari.
message: Nitrous needs Full Disk Access to import your Bookmarks from Safari.

name: IDS_IMPORT_FULL_DISK_ACCESS_DIALOG_LINK
context: Help link text in the dialog shown when Full Disk Access is needed to import from Safari.
message: Learn how to grant Full Disk Access from your System Preferences.

name: IDS_IMPORT_FULL_DISK_ACCESS_DIALOG_OPEN_SETTINGS
context: Accept button text for opening System Preferences from the Full Disk Access import dialog.
message: Open System Preferences

name: IDS_IMPORTED_FROM_BOOKMARK_FOLDER
context: Name for bookmark panel folder imported from another browser
message: Imported from <ph name="BROWSER_NAME">$1<ex>Nitrous</ex></ph>

name: IDS_EXTENSIONS_IMPORTER_LOCK_TITLE
context: Dialog title for importer lock dialog
message: Import profile still in use

name: IDS_EXTENSIONS_IMPORTER_LOCK_TEXT
context: The message to be displayed in the importer-lock dialog
message: To continue importing data, first close the browser you're importing from.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES
context: Name of the page which controls connectivity to Nitrous services
message: Nitrous services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_DESCRIPTION
context: Description of the controls available on the Nitrous services settings page
message: Manage what Nitrous services are allowed in your browser

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SCHEMA_NOTICE_TITLE
context: Title of the alert shown when Nitrous services have a change in behavior
message: Updates to Nitrous services:

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SCHEMA_NOTICE_DESCRIPTION
context: Description of the alert shown when Nitrous services have a change in behavior
message: These changes are not active until you dismiss this notice.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SCHEMA_IGNORE
context: Checkbox label to ignore any future services change alerts
message: Don't notify me again

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_TOGGLE
context: Global toggle for enabling/disabling of all Nitrous services
message: Allow connecting to Nitrous services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_TOGGLE_DESCRIPTION
context: Description of the toggle for enabling/disabling of all Nitrous services
message: When enabled, Nitrous will be able to connect to anonymous web services to provide additional functionality. When disabled, additional features will not work.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_OVERRIDE
context: Text input for overriding the Nitrous services server
message: Use your own instance of Nitrous services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_OVERRIDE_DESCRIPTION
context: Description of the text input for overriding the Nitrous services server
message: You can host your own instance of Nitrous services and use it in your browser instead of the default one. HTTPS only.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_OVERRIDE_DESCRIPTION_WARNING
context: Warning section of description for overriding the Nitrous services server
message: Do not use this field unless you know exactly what you are doing. Never paste URLs from other people here, they are trying to steal your data. We are not responsible for any damages caused by using a custom server and will not be able to help you.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_OVERRIDE_ARIA_LABEL
context: ARIA label of the text input for overriding the Nitrous services server
message: Custom origin URL for Nitrous services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HELIUM_SERVICES_OVERRIDE_INVALID_ORIGIN
context: Validation error shown when the custom Nitrous services origin is invalid.
message: Invalid origin

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERSONA_TITLE
context: Name of the page used to manage browser persona settings
message: Persona

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERSONA_DESCRIPTION
context: Description of the browser persona settings page
message: Configure browser persona and fingerprint surface overrides

name: IDS_APPMENU_TOOLTIP_HELIUM_SERVICES_UPDATE
context: The tooltip to show for the browser menu when Nitrous services schema changes
message: Nitrous services have been updated. Please review the changes.

name: IDS_HELIUM_SERVICES_SCHEMA_MENU_ITEM
context: Text for the bubble that notifies the user of a schema change in Nitrous services.
message: Review Nitrous services updates

name: IDS_APP_MENU_BUTTON_HELIUM_SERVICES_UPDATE
context: Short label next to app-menu button when Nitrous services behavior changes.
message: Services updated

name: IDS_PRODUCT_NAME
context: The Nitrous application name
message: Nitrous

name: IDS_SHORT_PRODUCT_NAME

name: IDS_BROWSER_WINDOW_TITLE_FORMAT

name: IDS_CAPTIVE_PORTAL_BROWSER_WINDOW_TITLE_FORMAT

name: IDS_PDF_INFOBAR_TEXT
context: Text to show in an infobar when Nitrous is not the default PDF viewer.
message: Set Nitrous as your default PDF viewer

name: IDS_RELAUNCH_CONFIRMATION_DIALOG_TITLE
context: The title of the modal dialog that we show when users click on relaunch and they have open Incognito windows. The dialog serves to warn the users about losing their Incognito windows if they proceed with the relaunch.
message: Relaunch Nitrous?

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ABOUT_PROGRAM
context: Menu title for the About Nitrous page.
message: About Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_GET_HELP_USING_CHROME
context: Text of the button which takes the user to the Nitrous help page.
message: Get help with Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_UPGRADE_UPDATING
context: Status label: Updating Nitrous
message: Updating Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_UPGRADE_UPDATING_PERCENT
context: Status label: Updating Nitrous (90%)
message: Updating Nitrous (<ph name="PROGRESS_PERCENT">$1<ex>90%</ex></ph>)

name: IDS_SETTINGS_UPGRADE_SUCCESSFUL_RELAUNCH
context: Status label: Successfully updated Nitrous
message: Nearly up to date! Relaunch Nitrous to finish updating.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_UPGRADE_UP_TO_DATE
context: Status label: Already up to date (Nitrous)
message: Nitrous is up to date

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CHECK_PASSWORDS_ERROR_OFFLINE
context: Error message when the password check can't be completed because the user is offline.
message: Nitrous can't check your passwords. Try checking your internet connection.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CHECK_PASSWORDS_ERROR_SIGNED_OUT
context: Error message when the password check can't be completed because the user is not signed in.
message: Nitrous can check your passwords when you sign in with your Google Account

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CHECK_PASSWORDS_ERROR_NO_PASSWORDS
context: Error message when the password check can't be completed since no passwords could be checked.
message: No saved passwords. Nitrous can check your passwords when you save them.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CHECK_PASSWORDS_ERROR_QUOTA_LIMIT_GOOGLE_ACCOUNT
context: Error message when the password check can't be completed since the user hit the quota limit, but the user is able to check their passwords in their Google account.
message: Nitrous can't check your passwords. Try again after 24 hours or <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="$1" target="_blank"></ph>check passwords in your Google Account<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CHECK_PASSWORDS_ERROR_QUOTA_LIMIT
context: Error message when the password check can't be completed since the user hit the quota limit.
message: Nitrous can't check your passwords. Try again after 24 hours.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CHECK_PASSWORDS_ERROR_GENERIC
context: Error message when the password check can't be completed for an unspecified reason.
message: Nitrous can't check your passwords. Try again later.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_NO_COMPROMISED_CREDENTIALS_LABEL
context: Label which is shown when there were no compromised passwords detected.
message: Nitrous will notify you when you sign in with a compromised password

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNED_OUT_USER_LABEL
context: Label which is shown at the top of the compromised password section if user is signed out.
message: To check if your passwords are safe from data breaches and other security issues, <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a target='_blank' href='$1'></ph>sign in to Nitrous<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNED_OUT_USER_HAS_COMPROMISED_CREDENTIALS_LABEL
context: Label which is shown at the bottom of the compromised password section if user is signed out and has compromised credentials.
message: To check if your other passwords are safe from data breaches and other security issues, <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a target='_blank' href='$1'></ph>sign in to Nitrous<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_WEAK_PASSWORDS_DESCRIPTION_GENERATION
context: Information about why weak passwords are a security issue and what syncing user should do to fix the problem. Has a link.
message: Weak passwords are easy to guess. Let Nitrous <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a target='_blank' href='$1'></ph>create and remember strong passwords for you<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_COMPROMISED_EDIT_DISCLAIMER_DESCRIPTION
context: A description for the dialog which tells the user to edit password in Nitrous if it was changed already.
message: If so, please edit your saved password in Nitrous so it matches your new password.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_AUTOFILL_AI_WHEN_ON_USE_TO_FILL
context: Bullet 2 of 2 that appears beneath the “When on” title. “Later”: this is a two-step feature. 1) data is saved, 2) it becomes valuable to the user later when they want to fill in a form with similar data. “Nitrous can ask”: the user has a choice, every time, whether their data is saved and also whether they want to autofill their saved info into forms.
message: Later, Nitrous can ask if you want to use saved info to autofill forms for you

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_DEFAULT
context: The text displayed when Nitrous is the default browser
message: Nitrous is your default browser

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_DEFAULT_THANK_YOU
context: The text displayed when Nitrous is the default browser
message: Thanks for choosing Nitrous as your default browser!

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_MAKE_DEFAULT
context: Default browser checkbox label
message: Make Nitrous the default browser

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_MAKE_DEFAULT_USER_VALUE
context: The text displayed when Nitrous is not the default browser. This string describes the effect (user benefit) of setting a browser as the default.
message: You can use Nitrous anytime you click links in messages, documents, and other apps

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_MAKE_DEFAULT_AND_PIN
context: The text of the button to make Nitrous the default browser and pin it to the taskbar.
message: Make Nitrous the default browser and pin to the taskbar

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_MAKE_DEFAULT_AND_PIN_USER_VALUE
context: The text of the button to make Nitrous the default browser and pin it to the taskbar.
message: You can use Nitrous anytime you click links in messages, documents, and other apps. It will also be pinned to your taskbar.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_ERROR
context: The text displayed when Nitrous cannot determine or set the default browser
message: Nitrous cannot determine or set the default browser

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_SECONDARY
context: The text displayed when Nitrous is installed in side-by-side mode, which does not support setting as the default browser.
message: This is a secondary installation of Nitrous, and cannot be made your default browser.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRELOAD_PAGES_STANDARD_PRELOADING_WHEN_ON_BULLET_TWO
context: Bullet point in the standard preloading and when on column. Informs the user about what the standard preloading setting does.
message: Nitrous preloads pages you're likely to visit, so that they load more quickly when you visit them

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRELOAD_PAGES_EXTENDED_PRELOADING_WHEN_ON_BULLET_TWO
context: Bullet point in the extended preloading and when on column. Informs the user about what the extended preloading setting does.
message: Nitrous preloads even more pages that you're likely to visit, so that they load more quickly when you visit them

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRELOAD_PAGES_EXTENDED_PRELOADING_THINGS_TO_CONSIDER_BULLET_TWO
context: Bullet point in the extended preloading and things to consider column. Informs the user about what the extended preloading setting does.
message: When a site asks to privately preload links on their page, Nitrous uses Google servers. This hides your identity from the preloaded site, but Google learns what sites get preloaded.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRELOAD_PAGES_THINGS_TO_CONSIDER_BULLET_ONE
context: Bullet point in the things to consider column. Informs the user about what the preloading setting does.
message: If you allow cookies, Nitrous may use them when preloading

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SPELLING_PREF_DESC
context: Description of using a web serviced to help resolve spelling errors. It is important to convey that what the user types will be sent to Google.
message: To fix spelling errors, Nitrous sends the text you type in text fields to Google

name: IDS_SETTINGS_RESTART_TO_APPLY_CHANGES
context: Description in the snackbar to restart Nitrous and apply changes.
message: To apply your changes, relaunch Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNIN_ALLOWED
context: The title of the preference to allow to sign-in to Nitrous
message: Allow Nitrous sign-in

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNIN_ALLOWED_DESC
context: The description of the preference to allow to sign-in to Nitrous
message: By turning this off, you can sign in to Google sites like Gmail without signing in to Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNIN_ALLOWED_DESC_2
context: The description of the preference to allow to sign-in to Nitrous
message: Shows prompts to sign in to Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SETTINGS_PDFS_BLOCKED
context: Label for the disabled option of the pdfs content setting.
message: Open PDFs in Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SITE_DETAILS_CAMERA_BLOCKED_BY_OS
context: The description for the camera permission with a link to system settings.
message: To use your camera, give Nitrous access in <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="openSystemSettingsLink" ></ph>system settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SITE_DETAILS_MICROPHONE_BLOCKED_BY_OS
context: The description for the microphone permission with a link to system settings.
message: To use your microphone, give Nitrous access in <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="openSystemSettingsLink" ></ph>system settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SITE_DETAILS_LOCATION_BLOCKED_BY_OS
context: The description for the location permission with a link to system settings.
message: To use your location, give Nitrous access in <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="openSystemSettingsLink" ></ph>system settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SETTINGS_CAMERA_BLOCKED_BY_OS
context: Warning for camera content setting when the camera is disabled on OS level.
message: To use your camera on these sites, give Nitrous access in <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="openSystemSettingsLink" ></ph>system settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SETTINGS_LOCATION_BLOCKED_BY_OS
context: Warning for location content setting when the camera is disabled on OS level.
message: To use your location on these sites, give Nitrous access in <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="openSystemSettingsLink" ></ph>system settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_SETTINGS_MIC_BLOCKED_BY_OS
context: Warning for microphone content setting when the camera is disabled on OS level.
message: To use your microphone on these sites, give Nitrous access in <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="openSystemSettingsLink" ></ph>system settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_DATA_PAGE_CLEAR_ON_EXIT_WITH_EXCEPTION_RADIO_SUBLABEL
context: A description of the benefit/cost of choosing 'Sites can save content on your device. In short, a site will remember your preferences, shopping cart content, etc. during this browser session. But once you close all open windows, and then visit the site again, it's as if you're coming to the site for the first time.
message: Sites will probably work as expected. You'll be signed out of most sites when you close all Nitrous windows, except your Google Account if you're signed in to Nitrous.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SITE_DATA_PAGE_DELETE_ON_EXIT_EXCEPTIONS_SUB_HEADING
context: 2 of 3. A label within 'Customized behaviors' that allows for exceptions to the 'On-device site data' setting. This label and the list of sites that can appear beneath it is only relevant if the user has chosen to allow 'on-device site data' as the default value above.
message: Always delete site data from your device when you close Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_PROMO_BODY
context: Body text of a card in the settings page that explains what the 'Privacy Guide' feature is.
message: Review key privacy and security controls in Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_SAFE_BROWSING_CARD_STANDARD_PROTECTION_FEATURE_DESCRIPTION2
context: A part of the feature description of the standard protection section of the safe browsing card in the 'Privacy Guide'.
message: Checks URLs with a list of unsafe sites stored in Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_SAFE_BROWSING_CARD_STANDARD_PROTECTION_PRIVACY_DESCRIPTION1
context: A part of the privacy description of the standard protection section of the safe browsing card in the 'Privacy Guide'.
message: If a site tries to steal your password, or when you download a harmful file, Nitrous may also send URLs, including bits of page content, to Safe Browsing

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_COMPLETION_CARD_PRIVACY_SANDBOX_SUB_LABEL
context: Text of the Privacy Sandbox sublabel in the completion card of the 'Privacy Guide'.
message: Nitrous gives you more control over the ads you see and limits what sites can learn about you when they show you personalized ads

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_COMPLETION_CARD_WAA_SUB_LABEL
context: Text of the Web and App Activity sublabel in the completion card of the 'Privacy Guide'.
message: Choose whether to include Nitrous history for more personalized experiences in Google services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_MSBB_FEATURE_DESCRIPTION3
context: A part of the feature description of 'make searches and browsing better' in the 'Privacy Guide'.
message: If you also save your bookmarks in your Google Account, you can track product prices in Nitrous and get notified when the price drops

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_GUIDE_MSBB_PRIVACY_DESCRIPTION2
context: A part of the privacy description of 'make searches and browsing better' in the 'Privacy Guide'.
message: If you also share Nitrous usage reports, those reports include the URLs you visit

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_SANDBOX_LEARN_MORE_DIALOG_TOPICS_TITLE
context: TOPICS API A sub title that describes the first of 2 ways a site can get info from Nitrous in order to target ads to the user. Topics of interest include things like “Live comedy” and “Rock music”. Nitrous estimates these interests based on the sites users visit. * “Your”: we need to convey a sense of ownership, even if the user didn’t explicitly choose the interests we’re talking about. Without the “your”, it could sound like Nitrous is simply building a generic library of interests. * “estimated”: It’s an “estimation”, and we don’t want to suggest that we know with certainty the user’s interests. Avoid words like “guess”, “establish”, “define”, etc. (in place of “estimate”). * “by Nitrous”: This is new behavior for a browser (Nitrous) to take an active role in processing user data for the purposes of showing ads. It’s important to convey “chrome”, the actor in this case.
message: Your interests as estimated by Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PRIVACY_SANDBOX_AD_MEASUREMENT_DIALOG_DESCRIPTION
context: A description of the Ad measurement API. * “Ad measurement” is the name of an API in this context. We don’t use “API” with the user, but they need to know that there is something called “A measurement” and what it does. * “sites you visit”: call the API to get information. The actor is the site, not Nitrous. * “restricts cross-site tracking”: Today, with 3rd-party cookies, ad measurement technologies can inadvertently (and intentionally) share considerable information from one site to another). This feature restricts that sharing in order to improve the user’s privacy.
message: Ad measurement allows sites you visit to request information from Nitrous that helps the site measure the performance of their ads. Ad measurement restricts cross-site tracking by transferring as little information as possible between sites.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_CHECK_UNUSED_SITE_PERMISSIONS_SETTING_SUBLABEL
context: By default Nitrous auto-revokes (disables) permissions (e.g. Camera, Location, etc.) for websites that were not visited for a while. This sublabel is for the toggle that allows users to switch off/on this functionality.
message: To protect your data, let Nitrous remove permissions from sites you haven't visited recently. Doesn't stop notifications.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_CHECK_UNUSED_SITE_PERMISSIONS_AND_NOTIFICATIONS_SETTING_SUBLABEL
context: By default Nitrous auto-revokes (disables) permissions (e.g. Camera, Location, etc.) for websites that were not visited for a while. This sublabel is for the toggle that allows users to switch off/on this functionality.
message: To protect your data, let Nitrous remove permissions from sites you haven't visited recently.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFEBROWSING_ENHANCED_BULLET_TWO
context: Bullet point under the safe browsing enhanced protection mode
message: Keeps you safe on Nitrous and may be used to improve your security in other Google apps when you are signed in

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFEBROWSING_STANDARD_BULLET_TWO
context: Bullet point under the safe browsing standard protection mode
message: Checks URLs with a list of unsafe sites stored in Nitrous. If a site tries to steal your password, or when you download a harmful file, Nitrous may also send URLs, including bits of page content, to Safe Browsing.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFEBROWSING_ENHANCED_WHEN_ON_BULLET_ONE
context: Bullet point under the Safe Browsing enhanced protection mode section titled 'When on'
message: Warns you about dangerous sites, even ones Google didn't know about before, by analyzing more data from sites than standard protection. You can choose to skip Nitrous warnings.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFEBROWSING_ENHANCED_LEARN_MORE_LABEL
context: The text for a link to a help center article that gives more information about Safe Browsing.
message: Learn more about <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="#" id="enhancedProtectionLearnMoreLink" on-click="onEnhancedProtectionLearnMoreClick_"></ph>how Nitrous keeps your data private<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SECURE_DNS_DESCRIPTION
context: Secondary, continued explanation of secure DNS in Privacy options
message: Make it harder for people with access to your internet traffic to see which sites you visit. Nitrous uses a secure connection to look up a site's IP address in the DNS (Domain Name System).

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYNC_DISCONNECT_DELETE_PROFILE_WARNING_WITH_COUNTS_SINGULAR
context: Warning message displayed in the Sign out of Nitrous dialog that indicates profile browsing data will be removed from the device.
message: This will delete 1 item from this device. To retrieve your data later, sign in to Nitrous as <ph name="USER_EMAIL">$1<ex>foo@example.com</ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYNC_DISCONNECT_DELETE_PROFILE_WARNING_WITH_COUNTS_PLURAL
context: Warning message displayed in the Sign out of Nitrous dialog that indicates profile browsing data will be removed from the device.
message: This will delete <ph name="ITEMS_COUNT">$1<ex>66</ex></ph> items from this device. To retrieve your data later, sign in to Nitrous as <ph name="USER_EMAIL">$2<ex>foo@example.com</ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYNC_DISCONNECT_DELETE_PROFILE_WARNING_WITHOUT_COUNTS
context: Warning message displayed in the Sign out of Nitrous dialog that indicates profile browsing data will be removed from the device.
message: This will delete your browsing data from this device. To retrieve your data later, sign in to Nitrous as <ph name="USER_EMAIL">$1<ex>foo@example.com</ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_CUSTOMIZE_YOUR_CHROME_PROFILE
context: Label of the link that takes you to the page to customize your chrome profile.
message: Customize your Nitrous profile

name: IDS_SETTING_NAME_YOUR_PROFILE
context: Title of the edit local profile name section on the manage profile page.
message: Name your Nitrous profile

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PEOPLE_SIGN_IN_PROMPT_SECONDARY_WITH_ACCOUNT
context: The secondary text displayed to prompt users to enable sync for an account that is alredy present in Nitrous.
message: Sync and personalize Nitrous across your devices

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PEOPLE_EXPLICIT_SIGN_IN_PROMPT_SECONDARY_WITH_NO_ACCOUNT
context: The secondary text displayed to prompt users to enable sync for an account that is already present in Nitrous.
message: To get your passwords and more on all your devices, sign in to Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PEOPLE_EXPLICIT_SIGN_IN_PROMPT_SECONDARY_WITH_NO_ACCOUNT_WITH_BOOKMARKS
context: The secondary text displayed to prompt users to enable sync for an account that is already present in Nitrous.
message: To get your passwords, bookmarks, and more on all your devices, sign in to Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PEOPLE_ACCOUNT_AWARE_SIGNIN_ACCOUNT_ROW_TITLE
context: The title of the avatar row shown to users who are signed in only to the web
message: Your Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ACCOUNT_MANAGER_DESCRIPTION_V2
context: Description of the Account Manager Settings page. Shown just below the title of the page.
message: You can manage your signed-in Google Accounts. Your Google Accounts are used for Nitrous browser, Play Store, Gmail, and more. If you want to add an account for someone else, like a family member, add a new person to your <ph name="DEVICE_TYPE">$1<ex>Chromebook</ex></ph> instead. <ph name="LINK_BEGIN"><a></ph>Learn more<ph name="LINK_END"></a></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNIN_CHROME_SIGNIN_OPTIONS_TITLE
context: Title of the settings that controls whether a user gets signed in to Nitrous when signing in to Google Services.
message: Sign in to Nitrous when you sign in to other Google services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGNIN_CHROME_SIGNIN_OPTIONS_DESC
context: Description of the settings that controls whether a user gets signed in to Nitrous when signing in to Google Services.
message: When signing into Google services like Gmail or Youtube with <ph name="USER_EMAIL">$1<ex>foo@example.com</ex></ph>, you can automatically sign in to Nitrous with the same account

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PEOPLE_SIGNIN_SECTION_SIGNED_OUT_TITLE
context: The text displayed to prompt users to sign in to Nitrous. Shown on top of the account card in the people settings page when the user is signed out.
message: Make Nitrous your own

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYNC_DATA_ENCRYPTED_TEXT
context: Text alerting the user that synced data is encrypted.
message: For added security, Nitrous will encrypt your data.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYNC_DISCONNECT_TITLE
context: The title of the dialog to stop syncing and sign out.
message: Sign out of Nitrous?

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SUGGEST_PREF_DESC
context: The description of the checkbox to enable/disable sending omnibox input to the user's default search engine to get additional suggestions.
message: When you type in the address bar or search box, Nitrous sends what you type to your default search engine to get better suggestions. This is off in Incognito.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SEARCH_AGGREGATOR_PREF_DESC
context: The description of the 'Show suggestions and results from your organization' checkbox, which enables/disables sending omnibox input to the user's organization's search aggregator to get additional suggestions. Note that this setting is controlled by enterprise policy and, while a checkbox is shown, it is always enabled and cannot be disabled.
message: When you type in the address bar or search box, Nitrous sends what you type to your organization's search, AI, and agent tools to get suggestions.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYNC_SIGN_IN_PROMPT_WITH_NO_ACCOUNT
context: The text displayed to prompt users to sign in to Nitrous.
message: Sign in to sync and personalize Nitrous across your devices

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERSONALIZE_GOOGLE_SERVICES_DESC
context: This is the subtitle of a setting that directs users to other settings where they can control how Nitrous history can be used to personalize other Google services. This subtitle appears in Nitrous's Advanced Sync settings, after the 'Control how browsing history is used with your other data across Google services' string. The 'Web & App Activity' text should match the branded term in Glossary Manager: https://localization.google.com/glossary/termset?gid=27517723&tsid=77d3b75.
message: To get personalization, include Nitrous in Web & App Activity

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERSONALIZE_GOOGLE_SERVICES_DESC_WITH_LINKED_SERVICES
context: This is the subtitle of a setting that directs users to other settings where they can control how Nitrous history can be used to personalize other Google services. This subtitle appears in Nitrous's Advanced Sync settings, after the 'Control how browsing history is used with your other data across Google services' string. The 'Web & App Activity' text should match the branded term in Glossary Manager: https://localization.google.com/glossary/termset?gid=27517723&tsid=77d3b75. The 'linked Google services' text should match the branded term in Glossary Manager: https://localization.google.com/glossary/termset?gid=27517723&tsid=517bace2.
message: To get personalization and other features, include Nitrous in Web & App Activity and linked Google services

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERSONALIZE_GOOGLE_SERVICES_LINKED_SERVICES_DESC
context: This is the subtitle of a setting that links to 'Linked Google services' in the Google Account's Data & privacy settings. This subtitle appears in Nitrous's Advanced Sync settings and is after the 'Linked Google services' string.
message: Link Nitrous and other Google services for personalization and other purposes

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ERROR_PASSPHRASE_USER_ERROR_DESCRIPTION
context: Subtitle of settings page that indicates user must re-enter their sync password.
message: To use and save Nitrous data in your Google Account, enter your passphrase

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ERROR_TRUSTED_VAULT_USER_ERROR_DESCRIPTION
context: Error description of the sync/signin error header of the settings page.
message: To use and save Nitrous data in your Google Account, verify it's you

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ERROR_UPGRADE_CLIENT_USER_ERROR_DESCRIPTION
context: Error message to resolve sync protocol error for out-of-date client.
message: To use and save Nitrous data in your Google Account, update Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PENDING_STATE_DESCRIPTION
context: Subtitle of settings page that indicates user is in pending state and should sign in.
message: Verify it's you to use and save Nitrous data in your account, <ph name="ACCOUNT_EMAIL">$1<ex>elisa.beckett@gmail.com</ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ERROR_PASSPHRASE_USER_ERROR_DESCRIPTION_WITH_EMAIL
context: Subtitle of settings page that indicates user must re-enter their sync password.
message: Enter your passphrase to use and save Nitrous data in your account, <ph name="USER_EMAIL">$1<ex>foo@example.com</ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SIGN_OUT
context: The text label of the Sign out of Nitrous button in the settings page.
message: Sign out of Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ACCOUNT_DATA_DASHBOARD
context: This string is found in the Account settings page in Nitrous settings to let a user manage the Nitrous data that they've saved in their Google Account. It is important that it is consistent with the destination's title 'Nitrous data in your account' (except using 'Nitrous' instead of 'Nitrous') (TC ID 2983573066282140756).
message: Nitrous data in your account

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ENCRYPT_ACCOUNT_DATA_WITH_PASSPHRASE_LABEL
context: Label for the radio button which, when selected, causes synced settings to be encrypted with a user-provided password.
message: Use your <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="$1" target="_blank"><ex><a href="$1" target="_blank"></ex></ph>own passphrase<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph> to encrypt all the Nitrous data in your Google Account. Payment methods and addresses from Google Pay won’t be encrypted. Browsing history from Nitrous won’t sync.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ENCRYPT_ACCOUNT_DATA_WITH_PASSPHRASE_EXPLANATION_TEXT
context: Message shown when explicit passphrase is selected.
message: Only someone with your passphrase can read your encrypted data. The passphrase is not sent to or stored by Google. If you forget your passphrase or want to change this setting, you’ll need to <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="$1" target="_blank"><ex><a href="$1" target="_blank"></ex></ph>delete the Nitrous data in your account<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PASSPHRASE_RESET_HINT_ACCOUNT_DATA_ENCRYPTION
context: Informs user how to change their encryption setting.
message: If you forgot your passphrase or want to change this setting, <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="$1" target="_blank"><ex><a href="$1" target="_blank"></ex></ph>delete the Nitrous data in your account<ph name="END_LINK"></a><ex></a></ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PAGE_CONTENT_THINGS_TO_CONSIDER_BULLET_TWO
context: Bullet point in the 'Things to consider' column of the description of the setting that enables features that provide contextual help for currently opened pages.
message: To help improve these features, Nitrous sends your interactions with them to Google. This data may be read, processed, and annotated by human reviewers.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_MEMORY_SAVER_MODE_SETTING_DESCRIPTION
context: Description for the memory saver mode setting
message: Nitrous frees up memory from inactive tabs. This gives active tabs and other apps more computer resources and keeps Nitrous fast. Your inactive tabs automatically become active again when you go back to them.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_BATTERY_SAVER_MODE_SETTING_DESCRIPTION
context: Description for the energy saver mode setting
message: Nitrous conserves battery power by limiting background activity and visual effects, such as smooth scrolling and video frame rates.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_PERFORMANCE_PRELOAD_TOGGLE_SUMMARY
context: Summary for the preload pages setting
message: Nitrous preloads pages which makes browsing and searching faster.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_LANGUAGES_IS_DISPLAYED_IN_THIS_LANGUAGE
context: The label for a language that is currently used as the UI display language.
message: This language is used to display menus, settings, alerts, and other text in Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_LANGUAGES_DISPLAY_IN_THIS_LANGUAGE
context: The label used for a button that changes the UI language.
message: Display Nitrous in this language

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SYSTEM_BACKGROUND_APPS_LABEL
context: Label for the checkbox to allow apps to run in the background, when Nitrous is closed.
message: Continue running background apps when Nitrous is closed

name: IDS_SETTINGS_RESET_PROFILE_FEEDBACK
context: Feedback label in the Reset Profile Settings dialog
message: Help make Nitrous better by reporting the <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a is="action-link" target="_blank"></ph>current settings<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_ENTRY_POINT_NOTHING_TO_DO
context: This text describes what Safety Hub does (regularly checking whether Nitrous settings are safe) and indicates whether the user should take action. This text is shown when Safety Hub has not identified any issues at this time. There is nothing to do for the user. As future checks of Safety Hub may identify issues, this state may change. This text is shown on a card in privacy settings next to a button that leads to Safety Hub.
message: Nitrous regularly checks to make sure your browser has the safest settings. We'll let you know if anything needs your review.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_ENTRY_POINT_HEADER
context: Title of a row in Nitrous's privacy settings (nitrous://settings/privacy) explaining that Nitrous recommends for the user to take action to improve their safety. Next to this text there is a button labeled 'Go to SafetyHub' that will take users to a dedicated page where users can take action.
message: Nitrous found some safety recommendations for your review

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_EMPTY_STATE_MODULE_SUBHEADER
context: The header of the 'Empty State Module' in the Safety Hub page. The 'Empty State Module' is visible if there is no safety recommendation.
message: Nitrous will let you know if anything needs your review

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_VERSION_MODULE_UPPERCASE_NAME
context: The entry point shown on the screenshot leads to Safety Check settings page (nitrous://settings/safetyCheck) when at least on of the SC modules have recommendations. The subheader of the entry point lists the modules with recommendations. This string is the upper case version (i.e. version that comes first in the sentence) for the name of Nitrous update module.
message: Nitrous update

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_VERSION_MODULE_LOWERCASE_NAME
context: The entry point shown on the screenshot leads to Safety Check settings page (nitrous://settings/safetyCheck) when at least on of the SC modules have recommendations. The subheader of the entry point lists the modules with recommendations. This string is the lower case version (i.e. version that comes in the middle of the sentence) for the name of Nitrous update module.
message: Nitrous update

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_PASSWORD_CHECK_SUBHEADER_NO_PASSWORDS
context: This text explains that passwords saved in Nitrous are automatically checked for issues (e.g. password is leaked or weak) by SafetyHub. However the user does not have any passwords stored in Nitrous, therefore the feature does not operate. This text is shown as a subtitle of the passwords card of SafetyHub. It sits below the title 'No saved passwords'.
message: Nitrous can check your passwords when you save them

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_USER_EDU_MODULE_HEADER
context: The header of the 'User Education Module' in the Safety Hub page. The 'User Education Module' is visible if there is no safety recommendation and gives information about some Nitrous features.
message: Learn how Nitrous keeps you safe

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_USER_EDU_DATA_HEADER
context: The header of the first item in the 'User Education Module' in the Safety Hub page. This item explains how Nitrous's tools help user to keep their data safe.
message: Nitrous's safety tools

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_USER_EDU_DATA_SUBHEADER
context: The subheader of the first item in the 'User Education Module' in the Safety Hub page. This item explains how Nitrous's tools help user to keep their data safe.
message: With <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="$1" target="_blank"></ph>tools from Nitrous<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph>, you can browse safely and stay in control

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_USER_EDU_INCOGNITO_HEADER
context: The header of the second item in the 'User Education Module' in the Safety Hub page. This item explains Incognito mode.
message: Nitrous Incognito

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_USER_EDU_SAFE_BROWSING_SUBHEADER
context: The header of the second item in the 'User Education Module' in the Safety Hub page. This item explains Safe Browsing.
message: <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a href="$1" target="_blank"></ph>Nitrous warns you<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph> about unsafe sites and downloads

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_VERSION_CARD_HEADER_RESTART
context: Safety Hub settings page (nitrous://settings/safetyHub) gives users an overview over their safety status. The version card, which this text is a part of, informs the user whether their Nitrous is up to date. This title of the card is shown when Nitrous is not up to date but there is an update available and the user needs to restart their browser to finish the update. Shown above the subtitle 'Relaunch to finish update. Your tabs will reopen'.
message: Nitrous is out of date

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_VERSION_CARD_HEADER_UPDATED
context: Safety Hub settings page (nitrous://settings/safetyHub) gives users an overview over their safety status. The version card, which this text is a part of, informs the user whether their Nitrous is up to date. This title of the card is shown when Nitrous is up to date and the user does not need to take any action. Shown above the subtitle that is the Nitrous version.
message: Nitrous is up to date

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_VERSION_NAVIGATION_ARIA_LABEL
context: Safety Check settings page (nitrous://settings/safetyCheck) gives users an overview over their safety status. The version card, which this text is a part of, informs the user whether their Nitrous is up to date. This string is for the accesibility label for the card to call out where to navigation will happen (nitrous://settings/help) if the card is clicked.
message: Go to About Nitrous page

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_VERSION_RELAUNCH_ARIA_LABEL
context: Safety Check settings page (nitrous://settings/safetyCheck) gives users an overview over their safety status. The version card, which this text is a part of, informs the user whether their Nitrous is up to date. This string is for the accesibility label for the card to call out the click on card will result restarting the browser.
message: Relaunch Nitrous

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_UNUSED_SITE_PERMISSIONS_REMOVED_ONE_PERMISSION_LABEL
context: 'Revoked Permissions' shows the revoked permissions of unused websites. This text describes which permission was removed from an unused site and is shown if there is exactly one permission.
message: You haven't visited recently. Nitrous removed <ph name="PERMISSION">$1<ex>location</ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_UNUSED_SITE_PERMISSIONS_REMOVED_TWO_PERMISSIONS_LABEL
context: 'Revoked Permissions' shows the revoked permissions of unused websites. This text describes which permissions were removed from an unused site and is shown if there are exactly two permissions.
message: You haven't visited recently. Nitrous removed <ph name="PERMISSION_1">$1<ex>location</ex></ph>, <ph name="PERMISSION_2">$2<ex>notifications</ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_UNUSED_SITE_PERMISSIONS_REMOVED_THREE_PERMISSIONS_LABEL
context: 'Revoked Permissions' shows the revoked permissions of unused websites. This text describes which permissions were removed from an unused site and is shown if there are exactly three permissions.
message: You haven't visited recently. Nitrous removed <ph name="PERMISSION_1">$1<ex>location</ex></ph>, <ph name="PERMISSION_2">$2<ex>notifications</ex></ph>, <ph name="PERMISSION_3">$3<ex>camera</ex></ph>.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_UNUSED_SITE_PERMISSIONS_REMOVED_FOUR_OR_MORE_PERMISSIONS_LABEL
context: 'Revoked Permissions' shows the revoked permissions of unused websites. This text describes which permissions were removed from an unused site and is shown if there are four or more permissions. The number indicates the count of permisions that are not shown explicitly. The number is always two or larger.
message: You haven't visited recently. Nitrous removed <ph name="PERMISSION_1">$1<ex>location</ex></ph>, <ph name="PERMISSION_2">$2<ex>notifications</ex></ph>, and <ph name="COUNT">$3<ex>2</ex></ph> more.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SAFETY_HUB_ABUSIVE_NOTIFICATION_PERMISSIONS_SETTING_SUBLABEL
context: 'Revoked Permissions' module shows the revoked permissions of websites. This text describes that abusive notification permissions were removed.
message: Dangerous site. Nitrous removed notifications.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SECURITY_PASSWORDS_LEAK_DETECTION_DESC
context: Text that briefly describes the 'Password Leak Detection' feature.
message: Get warned if Nitrous finds one of your passwords in a public data breach

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SECURITY_SAFE_BROWSING_STANDARD_HOW_IT_WORKS_CONTENT
context: Content of the Safe Browsing standard protection column describing how it works
message: When you visit a site, Nitrous sends an obfuscated portion of the URL to <ph name="BEGIN_LINK"><a target="_blank" href="https://safebrowsing.google.com/"></ph>Google Safe Browsing<ph name="END_LINK"></a></ph> through a privacy server that hides your IP address. If a site does something suspicious, full URLs and bits of page content are also sent.

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SECURITY_JAVASCRIPT_GUARDRAILS_UNRECOGNIZED_SITES_DESCRIPTION
context: Description of JavaScript Guardrails unrecognized sites option.
message: Enhanced protection turns off JavaScript optimization for sites Nitrous doesn’t recognize

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SECURITY_BUNDLE_ENHANCED_BULLET_ONE
context: First bullet point under the security settings enhanced bundle section
message: Turns on additional features to provide Nitrous’s strongest protections

name: IDS_SETTINGS_SECURE_CONNECTIONS_SECTION_DESCRIPTION
context: Description for the Secure Connections section in Security settings, explaining the baseline behavior of the browser even if the 'Always use secure connections' toggle is disabled.
message: Nitrous upgrades insecure connections to HTTPS automatically when possible

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HTTPS_FIRST_MODE_SUBTITLE
context: Subtitle for HTTPS-First Mode section in Security settings
message: Nitrous will try to upgrade navigations to HTTPS

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HTTPS_FIRST_MODE_ENABLED_FULL_SUBLABEL
context: Sublabel for radio button to fully enable HTTPS-First Mode
message: Nitrous will warn you before loading any site using an insecure connection

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HTTPS_FIRST_MODE_ENABLED_INCOGNITO_SUBLABEL
context: Sublabel for radio button to enable HTTPS-First Mode in Incognito
message: When browsing in Incognito mode, Nitrous will warn you before loading a site using an insecure connection

name: IDS_SETTINGS_HTTPS_FIRST_MODE_DISABLED_SUBLABEL
context: Sublabel for radio button to disable HTTPS-First Mode
message: When HTTPS isn't available, Nitrous will use an insecure connection without warning you

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ALT_PAGE_TITLE
context: The settings page title for the about page.
message: Settings - <ph name="SECTION_TITLE">$1<ex>About Nitrous</ex></ph>

name: IDS_SETTINGS_ABOUT_PAGE_BROWSER_VERSION
context: The text label describing the version of the browser, example: Version 57.0.2937.0-r123456 (Developer Build) unknown (64-bit). The suffix of the version (eg. '-r123456') exists only in limited cases.)
message: Version <ph name="HELIUM_PRODUCT_VERSION">$1<ex>0.0.0.0</ex></ph> (<ph name="PRODUCT_CHANNEL">$4<ex>Developer Build</ex></ph>, <ph name="CHROMIUM_NAME">$7</ph> <ph name="PRODUCT_VERSION">$2<ex>15.0.865.0</ex></ph><ph name="PRODUCT_VERSION_SUFFIX">$3<ex>-r123456</ex></ph>) <ph name="PRODUCT_MODIFIER">$5</ph> <ph name="PRODUCT_VERSION_BITS">$6</ph>

name: IDS_POLICY_SOURCE_HOP
context: Indicates that the policy originates from Nitrous defaults.
message: Nitrous defaults

name: IDS_CONTENT_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_TRANSLATE
context: The name of the macOS system Translate command in the content area context menu
message: Translate “<ph name="SELECTION_TEXT">$1<ex>Bonjour</ex></ph>”

name: IDS_SETTINGS_DEFAULT_BROWSER_SECONDARY
context: The text displayed when Helium is launched on Linux, but has no existing .desktop file.
message: Helium cannot be set as default, because no <ph name="DESKTOP_FILENAME">$1<ex>helium.desktop</ex></ph> file was found in any of: ~/.local/share/applications, /usr/share/applications, /usr/local/share/applications.
